name: haskell-migration
global_objective: Translate the Python forks/gizmo/gadget distributed developmental build system (scripts/forks/*.py, 22 modules) into a principled Haskell package under projects/lambda-script/src/lambda-script/lib/LambdaScript/Forks/, then make the Haskell tool self-hosting (used to drive its own remaining migration) before decommissioning the Python. Architecture is functional-core/imperative-shell with a single injected Git capability, typed wire formats with one canonical encoder, load-bearing invariants encoded as smart-constructor types (FreshCandidate, MaterialisedLedger), errors as values, and destructive operations isolated by namespace and gated by the replay materialisation audit. See accelerators/HASKELL_MIGRATION_DESIGN.md for the full rationale and Python->Haskell module map.
rules: Each agent keeps a distinct responsibility track.
For every turn, each agent maintains current implementation, next-slot complete plan, and plus-two lookahead.
Compile or verification failures enter an attempt loop for the same slot.
A slot advances only after every active agent has an accepted patch or is explicitly marked skipped.
JSON landing records replay history; lane propagation belongs to audited amalgamation.
Functional core, imperative shell: logic is pure; all git/process/fs effects live behind the MonadGit capability at the edge.
Every LS_FORK_*_V1 format is a typed record with a single canonical encoder; output must stay byte-identical to the Python during the parity phase.
Invariants are types, not asserts: pushing requires a FreshCandidate; a destructive lane rewind requires a MaterialisedLedger.
Pure planners (Plan.*, Accelerator, Workflow) are compiled without MonadGit in scope; Amalgamate is the only destructive module.
Do not delete any scripts/forks/*.py module until golden parity holds for it and the self-hosting smoke passes.

slots.objective: Shared spine first: land the contracts (typed wire formats, single error type, the injected Git capability, canonical hashing) and type-only skeletons for every track so parallel lanes have fixed signatures and a collision-free namespace partition before any behaviour is written.

ed.responsibility: Foundation & Git capability: shared data/format types, the single error type, the MonadGit interface, canonical hashing.
ed.lookahead: Slot 3: implement the forks CLI subcommands (diff/stage/verify/sync/capture) and the .forks dir layout over the capability.
ed.plan: Add Forks.Types (records + format tags for every LS_FORK_*_V1 the core touches) and Forks.Error (one ForksError sum). Define `class Monad m => MonadGit m` with gitRun/gitIn returning GitResult{exit,stdout,stderr} that never throws; refactor existing Git.hs IO into the IO instance. Keep Hash.canonicalJson as the one canonical encoder (sort_keys, compact separators) and prove it matches Python on a sample.
ed.patch_goal: Land Forks.Types + Forks.Error + the MonadGit capability (with IO instance) and confirm Hash.canonicalJson is byte-identical to Python json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')) on a fixture.
ed.notes: This is the spine every other track imports; freeze signatures here so later slots do not churn them.

edd.responsibility: Patch/Land/Ledger pipeline: the JSON-patch land path, replay ledger, submissions, agent-lane land.
edd.lookahead: Slot 3: Submission (LS_FORK_SUBMISSION_V1 capture/replay, non-destructive sync-lane) and AgentLand (the per-lane land path behind guy-land-json).
edd.plan: Re-express the already-faithful Patch + ReplayLedger and the Land engine to import Forks.Types/Error and the MonadGit capability instead of calling git directly; no behaviour change. Stub AgentLand/Submission signatures against the shared types.
edd.patch_goal: Land Patch/Land/ReplayLedger rewired onto Forks.Types/Error + MonadGit, behaviour unchanged, with AgentLand/Submission type-only skeletons.
edd.notes: Patch.hs and ReplayLedger.hs are already faithful (LS_FORK_JSON_PATCH_V1 upsert/delete, content-addressed ledger); this slot only aligns them to the spine.

eddy.responsibility: Gadget & Amalgamation: gadget branches, folder ingest, the audited amalgamation, promote, main-history, verify profiles.
eddy.lookahead: Slot 4: faithful amalgamate_all gadget mode (audit -> capture -> destructive rewind -> apply-to-integration -> re-audit -> assert-clean).
eddy.plan: Define type-only skeletons + signatures for GadgetBranches (integration_branch/gadget_agent_branch/status), Gadget ingest, Amalgamate, and MainHistory against Forks.Types. Encode the MaterialisedLedger newtype with no public constructor now so Amalgamate's signature can require it.
eddy.patch_goal: Land gadget/amalgamate/main-history type-only skeletons including the MaterialisedLedger smart-constructor type.
eddy.notes: Reserve the only destructive code to this track; its signatures must demand MaterialisedLedger before any rewind.

guy.responsibility: Planning & orchestration: the accelerator itself, replay/workflow planners, the unified CLI dispatcher and .bat->exe shims.
guy.lookahead: Slot 3: replay-plan and workflow-plan as pure planners (no MonadGit), wired into Dispatch.
guy.plan: Define Forks.Accelerator records + FromJSON/ToJSON mirroring LS_FORK_ACCELERATOR_V1 exactly (this file), with a round-trip test that re-encodes this request byte-identically. Sketch Cli/Dispatch with the subcommand enum.
guy.patch_goal: Land Forks.Accelerator data model that round-trips accelerators/haskell-migration.json without drift, plus the Cli/Dispatch skeleton.
guy.notes: Accelerator is compiled without MonadGit in scope; it is the bootstrap that lets the Haskell tool drive the rest of this plan.

slots.objective: Make the spine live: real Git/snapshot machinery, the green land/ledger pipeline on a golden fixture, gadget branch ref computation, and a working Haskell accelerator command set at parity with Python.

ed.lookahead: Slot 4: main_history (LS_MAIN_HISTORY_V1) and snapshot_diff_summary feed the amalgamation audit.
ed.plan: Implement git_manifest (LS_FORK_MANIFEST_V1 via git ls-tree + sha256), compact_snapshot {commit,tree,manifest_hash}, snapshots_match, candidate_identity, classify, ahead_behind, changed_files, merge_base, and guard_forbidden_paths over the capability. Match Python hashes exactly.
ed.patch_goal: Land Snapshot/Manifest with manifest_hash byte-identical to Python and the full git helper set on the IO MonadGit instance.
ed.notes: manifest_hash is the identity used by snapshots_match and the audit; parity here is non-negotiable.

edd.lookahead: Slot 4: replay-sync rebuilds a lane from the ledger non-destructively; share the materialisation-audit helper with eddy's Amalgamate.
edd.plan: Turn requireCandidateFresh into the FreshCandidate smart constructor (remote-first fetch + is-ancestor + ahead-only) and make pushRef require it. Keep appendEntry writing the content-addressed payload + per-agent ledger into the candidate before commit.
edd.patch_goal: Land path green end-to-end on a golden fixture, producing a ledger whose fingerprints match Python; push is reachable only with a FreshCandidate.
edd.notes: FreshCandidate is the first invariant-as-type; verify the ungated push path does not type-check.

eddy.lookahead: Slot 4: the destructive amalgamation core consumes these ref helpers and the status reader.
eddy.plan: Implement GadgetBranches pure ref computation (integration_branch=gadgets/<gizmo>/<gadget>/main, gadget_agent_branch=gadget-agents/<gizmo>/<gadget>/<agent>) and the status reader (per-lane ahead/behind/state) over the capability; init/sync remain non-destructive (even/behind-only).
eddy.patch_goal: Land GadgetBranches with init/status/sync at parity with gadget_branches.py on a live gadget.
eddy.notes: Ref string computation is pure and must match Python exactly so existing remote lanes resolve.

guy.lookahead: Slot 4: workflow_runner consumes accepted accelerator cells to emit land calls/packets for remote agents.
guy.plan: Implement the accelerator commands as pure state transforms (init/set-slot/set-agent/attempt/accept/advance) plus IO load/save at the canonical path, and the packet emitter (current + next + plus-two window). Enforce the advance gate and path-collision detection.
guy.patch_goal: Haskell `forks accelerator` command set at parity with accelerator.py, including packet output and the advance gate, validated against this request file.
guy.notes: Once this lands, the Haskell tool can read and advance this very plan -- the self-hosting bootstrap is reached.

slots.objective: Complete the capture/replay surface: forks CLI subcommands, submission objects + the per-agent land path, gadget folder ingest + creation orchestrator, and the pure replay/workflow planners.

ed.lookahead: Slot 5: gadget_verify_profiles wires optional cabal/npm gates into the verify command.
ed.plan: Implement the forks CLI subcommands (diff/stage/verify/sync/capture) and the .forks dir layout (patches/candidates/receipts/worktrees/snapshots/conflicts/ships) over the capability and Snapshot.
ed.patch_goal: Land the forks subcommands at parity with forks.py CLI for the non-destructive commands.
ed.notes: Keep destructive sync paths (reset --hard / branch -f) behind the same even/behind-only guard Python uses.

edd.lookahead: Slot 4: ReplaySync reuses Submission's capture and the ledger audit to realign lanes without losing work.
edd.plan: Implement Submission (LS_FORK_SUBMISSION_V1 capture/replay, sha-checked, non-destructive sync-lane) and AgentLand (the per-agent land path behind ed/edd/eddy/guy-land-json, including forbidden-path guard).
edd.patch_goal: Land Submission + AgentLand at parity; guy-land-json driven by the Haskell path produces an identical submission record to Python.
edd.notes: Submission patch_sha256 must verify on load exactly as submission_object.py does.

eddy.lookahead: Slot 4: the amalgamation core captures a gadget-submission per lane before any rewind.
eddy.plan: Implement gadget_ingest_folder (scan source folder -> diff vs dest on the lane -> emit LS_FORK_JSON_PATCH_V1 upsert/delete -> land) and the gadget creation orchestrator (ensure lane -> ingest -> optional amalgamate), with the default-excludes set.
eddy.patch_goal: Haskell gadget-creation ingest reproduces the copied/skipped/changed report and lands the same patch as gadget_creation_agent.py.
eddy.notes: Ingest is how this very migration is shipped; it must stay byte-faithful to the Python diff format.

guy.lookahead: Slot 5: the unified dispatcher routes every subcommand; .bat shims point at the wires-built exe.
guy.plan: Implement replay_plan and workflow_plan as pure planners (no MonadGit) that compute the intended sequence of land/sync/amalgamate steps, and wire them plus accelerator into Dispatch.
guy.patch_goal: Land Plan.Replay + Plan.Workflow pure planners with parity output to the Python plans, routed through Dispatch.
guy.notes: Planners must not import any effectful module; this is the structural destructive/non-destructive boundary.

slots.objective: Land the audited amalgamation (the only destructive core) plus the replay-sync and main-history it depends on, gated end-to-end by MaterialisedLedger.

ed.lookahead: Slot 6: provide golden-fixture helpers for the parity harness.
ed.plan: Implement main_history (LS_MAIN_HISTORY_V1) reader/writer and snapshot_diff_summary used by the amalgamation re-audit.
ed.patch_goal: Land MainHistory + snapshot_diff_summary at parity with main_history.py.

edd.lookahead: Slot 6: replay-sync underwrites the self-hosting smoke (lane realignment without loss).
edd.plan: Implement replay_sync (rebuild/realign a lane from the committed ledger non-destructively) and the shared materialisation-audit helper that yields MaterialisedLedger when every entry's fingerprints exist in the tree.
edd.patch_goal: Land ReplaySync + the audit helper; the audit refuses to produce MaterialisedLedger on a tampered fixture.
edd.notes: This audit is the second invariant-as-type and the precondition for any rewind.

eddy.lookahead: Slot 5: promote and verify-profiles complete the gadget lifecycle.
eddy.plan: Faithfully port amalgamate_all gadget mode: audit (before) -> per-agent capture gadget-submission -> destructive rewind lane to base (force-with-lease on source_commit) -> apply gadget patch to integration (git apply --3way --binary, force-with-lease) -> final sync -> re-audit -> assert-clean. Require MaterialisedLedger before the rewind step. Add the gadget_amalgamate_safe wrapper and amalgamate_targets resolution.
eddy.patch_goal: Haskell amalgamation reproduces the Python audit->rewind->apply order exactly and refuses to rewind without MaterialisedLedger.
eddy.notes: The 750-line beast; preserve the audit-before-and-after and force-with-lease semantics precisely -- this is where work is lost if done wrong.

guy.lookahead: Slot 6: the runner emits packets that task remote agents directly.
guy.plan: Implement workflow_runner: execute an accelerator/workflow plan by emitting per-agent packets and the land/sync/amalgamate calls in order, honouring the attempt loop and advance gate.
guy.patch_goal: Land Workflow runner that can drive this accelerator's remaining slots by emitting packets and invoking the land path.

slots.objective: Complete the gadget lifecycle (promote, verify profiles, creation orchestrator) and unify everything behind a single dispatcher and exe.

ed.lookahead: Slot 7: property tests for canonical JSON and the invariant types.
ed.plan: Implement gadget_verify_profiles (optional cabal/npm gates) and wire them into the verify command as opt-in, not default.
ed.patch_goal: Land VerifyProfiles as optional gates at parity with gadget_verify_profiles.py.

edd.lookahead: Slot 6: promote participates in the self-hosting cutover.
edd.plan: Implement gadget_promote (integration -> main) replay-safe, reusing the FreshCandidate gate.
edd.patch_goal: Land Promote at parity with gadget_promote.py, gated by FreshCandidate.

eddy.lookahead: Slot 6: gadget creation runs from the Haskell exe end-to-end.
eddy.plan: Finish the gadget creation orchestrator and unify land_json/gadget_land_json behind one land entry parameterised by target ref.
eddy.patch_goal: Single gadget-creation + land entry covering both main and gadget-integration targets.

guy.lookahead: Slot 6: flip the .bat shims to the wires-built exe.
guy.plan: Implement forks_dispatch as the single `lambda-script forks <command>` CLI routing all subcommands to pure planners + IO runners; expose it as the wires gadget exe.
guy.patch_goal: One dispatcher exe covering the whole command surface; .bat files reduced to thin shims (still pointing at Python until slot 6).

slots.objective: Self-hosting cutover: point the shims at the Haskell exe, drive a real land+amalgamate with it, and prove golden parity against Python.

ed.lookahead: Slot 7: convert the parity corpus into permanent regression tests.
ed.plan: Build the golden parity harness: fixed corpus of patches/lanes; assert canonical JSON, ledger fingerprints, and snapshot/manifest hashes are byte-identical Haskell-vs-Python.
ed.patch_goal: Parity harness green across the corpus.

edd.plan: Run the Haskell land path to land this gadget's next diff; confirm the ledger entry matches what Python would have written.
edd.patch_goal: Haskell tool lands its own next diff with a parity-checked ledger entry.

eddy.plan: Run the Haskell amalgamation (audit-gated) on a scratch gadget and assert-clean; compare against a Python run on the same inputs.
eddy.patch_goal: Self-hosting amalgamation smoke passes with identical end-state to Python.

guy.lookahead: Slot 7: remove Python once parity holds everywhere.
guy.plan: Flip the .bat shims (gadget-creation-*, *-land-json, amalgamate*, forks) to call the wires-built Haskell exe; keep a Python fallback flag for one slot.
guy.patch_goal: All entrypoints run the Haskell exe by default with a documented Python fallback.

slots.objective: Harden and decommission: property tests for determinism and the invariant types, documentation, and removal of scripts/forks/*.py once parity holds across the whole core.

ed.plan: Property tests: canonical-JSON determinism, fingerprint stability under re-encode, and that FreshCandidate/MaterialisedLedger cannot be constructed off the gated path.

edd.plan: Remove the now-replaced Python land/ledger/submission modules once their parity holds.
edd.notes: Delete only after parity per rule 10.

eddy.plan: Remove the Python gadget/amalgamate/main-history modules once their parity holds.
eddy.patch_goal: Python gadget/amalgamate modules deleted; references updated.
eddy.notes: Delete only after parity per rule 10.

guy.lookahead: Migration complete; the Haskell tool plans its own future work.
guy.plan: Remove the Python accelerator/workflow/dispatch modules, finalise docs, and update this accelerator to reflect completion. Final amalgamation/signover.
guy.patch_goal: Python core fully removed; Haskell tool is the sole forks build system; docs and this accelerator marked complete.
guy.notes: Signover slot: this is where amalgamation to integration/main is expected per the standing workflow rule.
